hypothesis: Incorporating a differentiable 100–200 step JAX-MD rollout loss that penalizes accumulated energy drift in addition to standard per-structure energy/force losses during training will reduce per-nanosecond total energy drift by at least 50% and at least double the fraction of stable 1 ns simulations on 1UAO and 1ABT relative to baseline MACE, NequIP, and ViSNet models trained on SPICE2 without rollout losses,
